title: Part 3 – Entraînement 2 : conversations finance, tech & transport
instructions: Écoutez chaque conversation, puis répondez aux trois questions.

items.audioText: Woman: I've been reviewing the Q2 expense reports and noticed that the travel budget was overspent by nearly twenty percent. Man: I know. We had three last-minute flights to Madrid that weren't budgeted for. Woman: We need to tighten the approval process. Going forward, any travel over five hundred euros should require my sign-off before booking. Man: Understood. I'll update the travel request form and send it to all team leaders by end of day.

questions.prompt: What issue has the woman identified?
choices: The Q2 revenue figures are incorrect
The travel budget was overspent by twenty percent
Three employees missed their flights to Madrid
The expense report deadline was missed
questions.explanation: "the travel budget was overspent by nearly twenty percent".

questions.prompt: Why was the budget exceeded?
choices: Staff used taxis instead of trains
Three last-minute flights to Madrid were not budgeted
questions.explanation: "We had three last-minute flights to Madrid that weren't budgeted for".

questions.prompt: What new rule does the woman want to introduce?
choices: All travel must be booked two weeks in advance
Travel over five hundred euros needs her approval before booking
Only managers can book international flights
All travel must be booked through the agency
questions.explanation: "any travel over five hundred euros should require my sign-off before booking".

items.audioText: Man: Our cloud storage costs have gone up forty percent this year. I think we need to audit what we're actually storing. Woman: Agreed. A lot of it is probably outdated project files that nobody accesses anymore. Man: Exactly. I'd suggest setting a retention policy — anything not accessed in two years gets archived or deleted. Woman: That makes sense. Let's get the IT team to run a usage report first so we know what we're dealing with.

questions.prompt: What problem are the speakers addressing?
choices: Slow internet connection in the office
A forty percent rise in cloud storage costs
questions.explanation: "Our cloud storage costs have gone up forty percent this year".

questions.prompt: What does the man suggest doing with files not accessed in two years?
choices: Backing them up to physical hard drives
Moving them to a cheaper server
questions.explanation: "anything not accessed in two years gets archived or deleted".

questions.prompt: What does the woman suggest doing first?
choices: Contacting the cloud provider for a discount
Getting IT to run a usage report
Deleting all files older than one year
questions.explanation: "Let's get the IT team to run a usage report first".

items.audioText: Woman: The company credit card statements came in. I'm seeing a charge of twelve hundred euros from a vendor called Orbex. Do you know what that's for? Man: Yes, that's the licence fee for the project management software we trialled last month. Woman: I don't remember approving that. Who authorised the purchase? Man: It was approved by the operations director as a pilot. But we've decided not to continue with it, so I'll request a refund today. Woman: Please do — and make sure any future software purchases go through the standard approval process.

questions.prompt: What is the woman questioning?
choices: A twelve-hundred-euro charge on the company credit card
A missing receipt for a business trip
A duplicate payment to a supplier
questions.explanation: "I'm seeing a charge of twelve hundred euros from a vendor called Orbex".

questions.prompt: What was the charge for?
questions.explanation: "that's the licence fee for the project management software we trialled last month".

questions.prompt: What will the man do today?
choices: Request a refund from the vendor
questions.explanation: "I'll request a refund today".

items.audioText: Man: The client's server went down at midnight and their team has been unable to access their data since. Woman: Did we get an alert? Man: Yes, our monitoring system flagged it at twelve-oh-five, but the on-call engineer didn't respond until one-thirty. Woman: That's unacceptable. Our SLA guarantees a fifteen-minute response time. We need to review our on-call procedures immediately. Man: I've already escalated it to the engineering director. We're meeting this afternoon to go over the incident.

questions.prompt: What happened at midnight?
choices: A power outage at the office
An employee lost access to the VPN
questions.explanation: "The client's server went down at midnight".

questions.prompt: Why is the woman dissatisfied?
choices: The monitoring system did not send an alert
The engineer responded too slowly, violating the SLA
The client was not informed about the outage
questions.explanation: "Our SLA guarantees a fifteen-minute response time" but the engineer didn't respond until one-thirty.

questions.prompt: What has the man already done?
choices: Sent an apology to the client
Escalated the issue to the engineering director
questions.explanation: "I've already escalated it to the engineering director".

items.audioText: Woman: I'm trying to book train tickets for the team trip to Brussels next month, but the corporate booking portal keeps crashing. Man: I had the same issue last week. Try clearing your browser cache — that fixed it for me. Woman: I already tried that. It's still not working. Man: In that case, call the travel desk directly. They can make the booking over the phone and send you the confirmation by email. Woman: All right, I'll do that now. Do you have their extension? Man: It's extension three-twenty.

questions.prompt: What problem is the woman experiencing?
choices: The train tickets are sold out
The team trip has been cancelled
The corporate booking portal keeps crashing
She cannot find the travel desk phone number
questions.explanation: "the corporate booking portal keeps crashing".

questions.prompt: What did the man suggest that the woman already tried?
choices: Logging in with a different account
questions.explanation: "Try clearing your browser cache" — "I already tried that".

questions.prompt: What is the travel desk extension?
questions.explanation: "It's extension three-twenty".

items.audioText: Man: The supplier just informed us they can't deliver the components until the fourteenth — that's two weeks later than planned. Woman: That's going to push back our whole production schedule. Can we source them from another supplier in the meantime? Man: I checked — the only alternative is twenty percent more expensive. Woman: Given the delay costs, it might still be worth it. Let me run the numbers and get back to you within the hour. Man: Please do. We need to make a decision before three o'clock.

questions.prompt: What problem has arisen?
choices: The supplier cannot deliver until two weeks later than planned
The production facility is closed for maintenance
A component order was placed incorrectly
questions.explanation: "they can't deliver the components until the fourteenth — that's two weeks later than planned".

questions.prompt: What is the disadvantage of using an alternative supplier?
questions.explanation: "the only alternative is twenty percent more expensive".

questions.prompt: By when does a decision need to be made?
questions.explanation: "We need to make a decision before three o'clock".

items.audioText: Woman: I've set up the new expense tracking software on the finance team's computers. Is everyone trained on it? Man: Most of the team did the online tutorial, but two people were out sick that day. Woman: I can run a short catch-up session for them — maybe thirty minutes on Thursday. Man: That would be great. Could you also send them the user guide PDF in the meantime? Woman: Of course, I'll email it to you and you can forward it.

questions.prompt: What has the woman just set up?
questions.explanation: "I've set up the new expense tracking software on the finance team's computers".

questions.prompt: Why haven't two team members been trained yet?
choices: They refused to take the tutorial
They were out sick during the training
They were on a business trip
questions.explanation: "two people were out sick that day".

questions.prompt: What will the woman send to the man?
choices: The login credentials for the software
questions.explanation: "I'll email it to you and you can forward it" (the user guide PDF).

items.audioText: Man: I'm looking at the flight options for the trade fair in Tokyo. There are two viable routes — a direct flight at eight hundred euros or a connecting flight via Dubai at five hundred and fifty. Woman: What's the travel time difference? Man: The direct flight is eleven hours. Via Dubai it's about sixteen hours with a three-hour layover. Woman: For a trip that long, I'd go with the direct flight. The extra cost is justified by the shorter journey time. Man: I agree. I'll book the direct flight for both of us.

questions.prompt: What are the speakers comparing?
choices: Two airlines serving the same route
questions.explanation: They're comparing a direct flight and a connecting flight to Tokyo.

questions.prompt: How long is the connecting flight via Dubai including the layover?
questions.explanation: "about sixteen hours with a three-hour layover".

questions.prompt: Which option do they decide on?
choices: The connecting flight to save money
The direct flight for a shorter journey time
They will decide after checking again
questions.explanation: "I'd go with the direct flight. The extra cost is justified by the shorter journey time".

items.audioText: Woman: We're moving to a new CRM system at the end of the quarter. I want to make sure the data migration goes smoothly. Man: Have you backed up everything in the current system? Woman: Yes, our database administrator did a full backup last Friday. Man: Good. Who'll be managing the migration on the technical side? Woman: Our IT project manager, with support from the CRM vendor's implementation team. They'll work over the weekend to minimise disruption. Man: Smart. Make sure users get a briefing on the new interface before Monday.

questions.prompt: What is happening at the end of the quarter?
choices: The company is merging with a competitor
A new CRM system is being introduced
The IT department is being restructured
A new data protection policy is launching
questions.explanation: "We're moving to a new CRM system at the end of the quarter".

questions.prompt: Who will manage the technical migration?
choices: The IT project manager and the CRM vendor's team
The finance and IT departments jointly
questions.explanation: "Our IT project manager, with support from the CRM vendor's implementation team".

questions.prompt: What does the man recommend before Monday?
choices: Testing the new system with a small group
Briefing users on the new interface
Delaying the migration by one week
questions.explanation: "Make sure users get a briefing on the new interface before Monday".

items.audioText: Man: The company van needs servicing. The last service was over fifteen thousand kilometres ago. Woman: Is it due for an MOT as well? Man: Yes, both are due. I've contacted three garages for quotes. The cheapest was four hundred and twenty euros all in. Woman: That's reasonable. Go ahead and book it. Try to get it done on a day when we don't have scheduled deliveries. Man: Tuesday looks quiet. I'll book it in for Tuesday morning.

questions.prompt: What does the company van need?
choices: New tyres and a paint job
A full inspection after an accident
questions.explanation: "The last service was over fifteen thousand kilometres ago" and the MOT is also due.

questions.prompt: How much is the cheapest quote?
questions.explanation: "The cheapest was four hundred and twenty euros all in".

questions.prompt: When will the man book the service?
questions.explanation: "Tuesday looks quiet. I'll book it in for Tuesday morning".
